title: 10 best herbal tinctures for puffy eyes
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for puffy eyes are concentrated liquid extracts of healing herbs, which are used topically to reduce eye puffiness. They are a natural and effective alternative to commercial eye creams. Common herbal tinctures for puffy eyes include chamomile, witch hazel, and calendula. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the skin, reducing puffiness. Witch hazel is an astringent that tightens the skin, while calendula improves blood circulation, reducing fluid buildup. By using these herbal tinctures, individuals can alleviate puffy eyes, looking refreshed and revitalized. This can improve their self-confidence and overall quality of life, as they no longer have to feel self-conscious about their appearance.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arnica flower tincture helps with puffy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and inflammation in the eye area. Arnica contains helenalin and dihydrohelenalin, which are compounds that help to stimulate the flow of blood and reduce fluid buildup, thereby alleviating puffiness. Additionally, arnica has been shown to have a soothing effect on the skin, which can help to reduce redness and irritation. Its natural astringent properties can also help to tighten the skin around the eyes, making it look more refreshed and revitalized. Overall, arnica flower tincture is a gentle and effective natural remedy for reducing puffy eyes and promoting healthy-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Arnica flower tincture reduces inflammation and swelling in the eye area, providing relief from puffiness. This property is due to the presence of helenalin and dihydrohelenalin, which inhibit the production of prostaglandins that cause inflammation.

Vasoconstrictor: Arnica flower tincture constricts blood vessels, reducing the appearance of bloodshot eyes and puffiness. This is due to the presence of lactones, which cause the constriction of blood vessels.

Analgesic: Arnica flower tincture helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with puffy eyes. This property is attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, which have pain-relieving effects.

Diuretic: Arnica flower tincture promotes fluid drainage, reducing the accumulation of fluid in the eye area and alleviating puffiness. This is due to the presence of essential oils, flavonoids, and tannins, which have diuretic properties.

Antioxidant: Arnica flower tincture protects the skin from oxidative stress, preventing damage to the delicate skin around the eyes and reducing puffiness. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which neutralize free radicals and protect the skin from damage.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 ounces of dried arnica flowers and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or rum (80 proof) over the arnica flowers, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it daily.
After the designated time, strain the mixture using a cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid from the flowers.
Store the final tincture in a dark glass bottle, keeping it in a cool, dark place for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with puffy eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and puffiness. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, help improve blood circulation and strengthen blood vessels, which can alleviate the appearance of puffy eyes. Additionally, pot marigold has antimicrobial and astringent properties that can help soothe and tighten the skin around the eyes, reducing the likelihood of fluid retention and puffiness. Its calming effect can also help decrease eye strain and fatigue, providing a refreshed and revitalized appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for puffy eyes as it helps alleviate swelling and redness. This property is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and carotenoids.

Astringent: It has astringent properties that tighten the skin, which is beneficial for puffy eyes as it can help reduce the appearance of bags under the eyes. This is due to the tannins present in the tincture.

Diuretic: Pot marigold tincture helps eliminate excess fluid, which is crucial for puffy eyes as it aids in reducing fluid retention around the eye area. The diuretic effect is attributed to the saponins found in the tincture.

Antimicrobial: It inhibits microbial growth, which is significant for puffy eyes as it can help prevent eye infections that may cause or worsen puffiness. The antimicrobial properties are primarily due to the presence of volatile oils.

Soothing: Pot marigold tincture has calming effects on the skin, which is important for puffy eyes as it helps alleviate irritation and discomfort. This soothing effect is attributed to the plant's mucilage content.
remedy_recipe: Harvest pot marigold flowers at peak bloom, ideally in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Fill a clean glass jar with roughly 2:1 ratio of fresh flowers to 100-proof vodka, ensuring flowers are completely submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, discarding the spent flowers.
Store the resulting tincture in a sterilized glass container, in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Apply a small amount to a cotton ball and gently dab around the eye area for puffy eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tincture helps with puffy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and puffiness. The active compounds in chamomile, such as bisabolol and chamazulene, help to constrict blood vessels and decrease fluid retention around the eyes. Additionally, chamomile has soothing and calming effects, which can alleviate irritation and redness. By applying a small amount of diluted chamomile tincture to a cotton ball and gently dabbing it onto the affected area, you can experience relief from puffy eyes and promote overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for puffy eyes as it helps to decrease swelling and redness. This property is attributed to the active constituent, chamazulene.

Antioxidant: It contains antioxidants that can help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the appearance of puffiness. The antioxidant properties come from flavonoids and terpenoids present in the tincture.
Anti-emphatic: German chamomile tincture has an anti-emphatic effect, which helps to soothe and calm the skin, reducing puffiness and irritation. This is due to the active constituent, matricin.
Astringent: It has a mild astringent effect, which helps to tighten and tone the skin, reducing the appearance of puffy eyes. This property is attributed to the tannins present in the tincture.
Analgesic: German chamomile tincture has analgesic properties, which can help to relieve any discomfort or pain associated with puffy eyes. This is due to the active constituent, bisabolol.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh German chamomile flowers, ideally in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Coarsely chop the flowers and place them in a clean glass jar, then add 8 ounces of 80-proof vodka or rum.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the extraction period, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, discarding the plant material.
Transfer the resulting tincture to a clean glass dropper bottle, storing it in a cool, dark location; use 1-2 drops under each eye as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tincture helps with puffy eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can reduce swelling and irritation. The herb, also known as Euphrasia officinalis, has been used traditionally to soothe tired and irritated eyes. Its astringent properties help to tighten the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of puffiness, while its anti-inflammatory effects can help to alleviate any redness or swelling. Additionally, eyebright tincture contains antioxidants that can help to protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. All of these properties make eyebright tincture an effective natural remedy for reducing puffy eyes and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eyebright tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for puffy eyes as it helps decrease swelling and redness. The active constituents responsible for this property include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and tannins.

Astringent: Eyebright tincture has astringent properties that tighten skin and reduce fluid retention, making it helpful for puffy eyes by constricting blood vessels and minimizing the appearance of swelling. The astringency is attributed to the tannins present in eyebright.

Antimicrobial: Eyebright tincture inhibits bacterial growth, which is important for puffy eyes because it can help prevent eye infections and reduce eye inflammation. The antimicrobial properties are due to the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: Eyebright tincture contains antioxidants that protect cells from damage, which is important for puffy eyes as it aids in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation. The antioxidant activity is attributed to the high levels of flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Diuretic: Eyebright tincture promotes diuresis, which helps eliminate excess fluid from the body, making it beneficial for puffy eyes by reducing fluid retention around the eye area. The diuretic effect is primarily due to the tannins present in eyebright.
remedy_recipe: Harvest Eyebright herb (Euphrasia spp.) at peak bloom, filling a clean, dry jar halfway with fresh or dried aerial parts.
Add 80 proof or higher drinking alcohol (such as vodka) to cover the herb, leaving a 1-inch headspace.
Cap the jar, label it with the plant name, date, and alcohol percentage, then store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking weekly.
Strain the tincture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the marc and discarding it.
Store the finished Eyebright tincture in a glass dropper bottle, away from light and heat, for up to 2 years. Adult dosage is 30-60 drops in water, 2-3 times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel tincture helps with puffy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and tighten the skin. It contains tannins, which can help to constrict blood vessels and decrease the buildup of fluid in the eye area. Additionally, its astringent properties can help to tone and firm the skin, reducing the appearance of puffiness. Witch hazel is also naturally soothing and can help to alleviate irritation and redness. Its natural ingredients make it a gentle and effective option for reducing puffy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Witch hazel tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce puffiness in eyes by constricting blood vessels and decreasing swelling, due to the active constituents tannins and gallic acid.
Astringent: Its astringent properties tighten and tone the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of puffiness, thanks to the high tannin content.

Antioxidant: Witch hazel tincture's antioxidant properties protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage and inflammation, contributing to the reduction of puffiness, due to the presence of flavonoids and proanthocyanidins.
Soothing: The soothing properties of witch hazel tincture alleviate irritation and redness, helping to minimize the appearance of puffy eyes, which can be attributed to the tannins and volatile oils.
Mild analgesic: Witch hazel tincture's mild analgesic properties help relieve discomfort associated with puffy eyes, thanks to the active constituents such as tannins and volatile oils.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 oz of dried witch hazel leaves and bark into small pieces, then combine with 1 pint of 80-proof vodka in a clean jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily.
After the allotted time, strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out excess liquid.
Discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool, dark place for up to a year.
To use, apply a small amount of the tincture to a cotton ball and gently dab under the eyes to reduce puffiness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with puffy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and puffiness. The herb contains several active compounds, including hypericin and hyperforin, which have been shown to decrease inflammation and improve circulation. When applied topically, St. John's wort tincture can help stimulate blood flow and reduce fluid buildup in the eye area, leading to a more refreshed and awake appearance. Additionally, its antioxidant properties can help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by environmental stressors, further contributing to a brighter and more youthful appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for puffy eyes as it helps alleviate swelling and redness. The active constituents responsible for this property are hypericin and hyperforin.

Antioxidant: It contains antioxidants that help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage, preventing further puffiness and dark circles. The main antioxidants are hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.
Astringent: St. John's wort tincture has astringent properties that help tighten the skin, reducing the appearance of puffy eyes. Tannins present in the tincture provide this astringent effect.

Analgesic: It has pain-relieving properties that can alleviate any discomfort or sensitivity associated with puffy eyes. Hypericin and hyperforin are the active constituents responsible for this analgesic effect.

Circulation-boosting: St. John's wort tincture improves blood circulation, which helps decrease puffiness by reducing fluid buildup in the eye area. The flavonoids and essential oils in the tincture contribute to this circulation-boosting property.
remedy_recipe: Harvest St. John's Wort flowers and leaves, filling a clean jar two-thirds full.
Pour 100 proof vodka over the herbs until they are completely submerged, screwing on a lid.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for four to six weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean jar, squeezing out the excess.
Label the jar with the contents and date, storing it in a cool, dark place for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tincture helps with puffy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and irritation. The herb contains bioactive compounds like flavonoids, tannins, and saponins that have a soothing effect on the skin, helping to alleviate puffiness and dark circles. Additionally, plantain has astringent properties that can tighten the skin, reducing the appearance of puffy eyes. Its high vitamin C content also promotes collagen production, which can improve skin elasticity and firmness around the eyes. Regular use of plantain tincture can help maintain healthy and refreshed-looking eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tincture helps reduce inflammation, which is important for puffy eyes as it can alleviate swelling and redness caused by fluid retention. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, tannins, and mucilage.

Astringent: Its astringent properties help tighten and tone the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of puffiness. This is due to the tannins present in plantain, which have a shrinking effect on tissues.

Antihistamine: Plantain tincture can help alleviate allergic reactions that can lead to puffy eyes. The active constituent quercetin acts as a natural antihistamine, reducing allergy-induced inflammation.

Diuretic: Plantain tincture promotes fluid elimination from the body, which is important for reducing puffiness caused by fluid retention. The potassium and magnesium content in plantain contribute to its diuretic properties.
Analgesic: Plantain tincture has pain-relieving properties, which can soothe any discomfort associated with puffy eyes. The active constituent allantoin is responsible for this analgesic effect.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 large plantain leaves, rinse, and chop into small pieces.
Combine leaves with 1 cup of vodka or rum in a clean jar, ensuring leaves are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, gently shaking every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, discarding the spent plant material.
Store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle and apply a few drops under the eyes as needed for puffiness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Butcher's broom tincture helps with puffy eyes because it contains ruscogenins, which have anti-inflammatory and vasoconstrictive properties. These properties can help reduce swelling and tighten the blood vessels around the eyes, thereby alleviating puffiness. Additionally, butcher's broom has been shown to improve circulation and strengthen the walls of blood vessels, which can further reduce the appearance of puffy eyes. Overall, butcher's broom tincture is a natural and effective remedy for reducing puffiness and promoting healthy-looking eyes.
remedy_properties: Venotonic effect: Butcher's broom tincture helps reduce puffy eyes by constricting veins and improving blood flow, thanks to its active constituents, ruscogenins.
Anti-inflammatory properties: It helps decrease inflammation and swelling in the eye area, thanks to its flavonoids and saponins.
Diuretic effect: Butcher's broom tincture promotes fluid elimination, reducing excess fluid accumulation in the eye area, due to its steroidal saponins.

Antioxidant properties: It protects the delicate skin around the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, preserving its elasticity and appearance, thanks to its high concentration of ruscogenins and flavonoids.
Vasoprotective effect: By strengthening and protecting blood vessels, it reduces the likelihood of leakage and swelling in the eye area, due to its ruscogenins and saponins.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried butcher's broom root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or grain alcohol (80-100 proof) over the herb, ensuring it is completely submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh sieve, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Store the final tincture in a clean, amber glass dropper bottle for up to 2 years, using 1-2 drops under the eyes as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tincture helps with puffy eyes because it contains allantoin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, it can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eye area. Additionally, comfrey tincture is rich in vitamin C and other antioxidants that can improve circulation and promote the drainage of excess fluids, further reducing puffiness. Its astringent properties can also help tighten the skin, making the eyes appear more refreshed and rejuvenated. Regular use of comfrey tincture can help maintain the health and vitality of the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of puffiness and dark circles.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and puffiness in the eyes by decreasing inflammation, due to the presence of allantoin and rosmarinic acid.
Astringent: Its astringent property tightens the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of puffiness, thanks to tannins present in comfrey.
Analgesic: Comfrey tincture's analgesic property helps alleviate any discomfort or pain associated with puffy eyes, owing to the presence of phenolic acids like rosmarinic acid.

Promotes tissue repair: Comfrey tincture aids in the repair of damaged tissues around the eyes, reducing the appearance of puffiness, due to allantoin, a cell proliferant that promotes new tissue growth.
Improves circulation: Comfrey tincture enhances blood flow and microcirculation, which helps in the drainage of excess fluids from the eye area, thanks to the presence of saponins and essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried comfrey root into small pieces and place in a clean jar.
Pour 1 pint of 100-proof vodka over the chopped comfrey root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle and apply a small amount to a cotton ball, gently dabbing around puffy eyes as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with puffy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and irritation. The herb contains flavonoids, such as luteolin and isorhamnetin, which have been shown to help decrease inflammation in the body. Additionally, yarrow tincture can improve circulation, which can help drain excess fluid from the eye area, further reducing puffiness. Its astringent properties can also help tighten the skin around the eyes, making them look less swollen and tired. Regular use of yarrow tincture can help maintain healthy and refreshed-looking eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and puffiness in eyes by constricting the blood vessels and reducing fluid buildup, thanks to active constituents like lactones, flavonoids, and tannins.
Astringent: The astringent properties of yarrow tincture tighten and tone the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of puffiness, due to the presence of tannins.
Diuretic: Yarrow tincture promotes fluid excretion, helping to alleviate fluid retention and puffiness in the eye area, attributed to the flavonoids and volatile oils found in yarrow.

Analgesic: Yarrow tincture's pain-relieving properties can soothe any discomfort associated with puffy eyes, as active constituents like alkaloids, lactones, and flavonoids work together to provide relief.

Antimicrobial: Yarrow tincture's antimicrobial properties help protect the eye area from potential infections, which can contribute to puffiness, thanks to the presence of volatile oils and flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, chop finely, and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or rum (80 proof) over the chopped yarrow, ensuring all plant material is submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the plant material.
Store the finished yarrow tincture in a tightly sealed glass container, in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Apply a few drops under the eyes as needed for puffiness.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat puffy eyes with herbal tinctures is by using a chamomile or eyebright tincture. Soak a cotton ball in the tincture and apply it gently to the affected area for 10-15 minutes. These herbs have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and puffiness. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day for best results. Remember to always do a patch test first to ensure no skin irritation occurs.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to puffy eyes that are treated with herbal tinctures include eye strain, dark circles, and inflammation. Eyebright tincture is commonly used to soothe eye strain and reduce puffiness, while goldenseal tincture can help reduce inflammation and improve overall eye health. For dark circles, a tincture made from bilberry can help improve blood flow and reduce discoloration. It is important to consult with a healthcare provider before using any herbal tinctures to ensure safety and effectiveness.
